Benchmark.title: STIG Baseline for nginx Container Image
Benchmark.description:
This STIG baseline template applies DISA Security Technical Implementation Guide controls to the nginx container image (Debian Bookworm with Nginx 1.27.3 and wolfSSL FIPS).
Container Scope: - Application runtime security - Cryptographic enforcement - Audit logging - Access controls - Package management
Host Responsibilities (Out of Scope): - Kernel hardening - Boot process security - Physical security - Host network configuration - Host-level services

Profile.title: Container FIPS Baseline
Profile.description: Baseline profile for FIPS-compliant containerized applications on Debian Bookworm. Includes only controls applicable to container runtime environments.

Group.title: SRG-OS-000033-GPOS-00014
Group.description: The Debian operating system must implement FIPS-validated cryptographic mechanisms.

Rule.title: Debian must use FIPS-validated cryptographic mechanisms.
Rule.description:
Use of weak or untested encryption algorithms undermines the purposes of utilizing encryption to protect data. The operating system must implement cryptographic modules adhering to the higher standards approved by the federal government.
Container Implementation: - wolfSSL FIPS v5.8.2 (Certificate #4718) - Nginx 1.27.3 with SSL module - wolfProvider v1.1.0 - OpenSSL 3.0+ provider routing to wolfSSL FIPS - OpenSSL 3.0.19 provider interface with FIPS property filtering

check.check-content:
Verify FIPS mode is enabled in the container:
1. Check OpenSSL configuration: $ docker exec <container> cat /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf | grep -A10 "fips_sect"
Expected output: [fips_sect] activate = 1 module = /usr/local/openssl/lib64/ossl-modules/wolfprov.so fips = yes
[algorithm_sect] default_properties = fips=yes
2. Verify OpenSSL provider status: $ docker exec <container> openssl list -providers
Expected: wolfSSL Provider FIPS v1.1.0 (status: active)
3. Check container startup logs for FIPS POST: $ docker logs <container> 2>&1 | grep "FIPS POST"
Expected: ✓ FIPS POST completed successfully
4. Test TLS connection with FIPS cipher: $ echo "Q" | openssl s_client -connect localhost:443 -tls1_3 2>&1 | grep "Cipher"
Expected: Cipher is TLS_AES_256_GCM_SHA384
If FIPS provider is not configured or validation fails, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Ensure container is built with FIPS-validated cryptographic modules:
1. Use wolfSSL FIPS v5.8.2 (Certificate #4718) 2. Build and install wolfProvider v1.1.0 (OpenSSL provider) 3. Configure /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf: - providers = provider_sect - fips = fips_sect - activate = 1 - module = /usr/local/openssl/lib64/ossl-modules/wolfprov.so - default_properties = fips=yes (CRITICAL: enables FIPS property filtering) 4. Configure nginx with ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3 5. Configure nginx with FIPS-approved cipher suites only 6. Verify FIPS POST passes on startup
Rebuild container if FIPS mode is not properly configured.

check.fixtext: Configure FIPS mode for the container

Group.title: SRG-OS-000396-GPOS-00176
Group.description: The Debian operating system must block non-FIPS approved cryptographic algorithms.

Rule.title: Debian must block MD5 and weak cryptographic hash functions.
Rule.description:
Unapproved mechanisms that are used for authentication to the cryptographic module are not verified and therefore cannot be relied upon to provide confidentiality or integrity.
Container Implementation: - MD5: Blocked at OpenSSL EVP API level via FIPS property filtering (default_properties=fips=yes) - 3DES, RC4, DES: Blocked by wolfProvider (0 cipher suites available) - TLS 1.0/1.1: Blocked (cannot negotiate - MD5-SHA1 digest unavailable) - SHA-1 for new TLS: Blocked (0 SHA-1 cipher suites) - SHA-1 for verification: Available (legacy cert verification - FIPS 140-3 compliant) - Enforcement layer: OpenSSL 3.0.19 provider + nginx configuration

check.check-content:
Verify non-FIPS algorithms are blocked:
1. Test MD5 blocking at OpenSSL level: $ docker exec <container> bash -c "echo -n 'test' | openssl dgst -md5"
Expected: Error setting digest
2. Check nginx SSL protocols configuration: $ docker exec <container> cat /etc/nginx/nginx.conf | grep ssl_protocols
Expected: ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3;
3. Test TLS 1.0 connection (should fail): $ echo "Q" | timeout 3 openssl s_client -connect localhost:443 -tls1 2>&1 | grep "Cipher"
Expected: Cipher is (NONE)
4. Check available FIPS cipher suites: $ docker exec <container> cat /etc/nginx/nginx.conf | grep ssl_ciphers
Expected: 14 FIPS-approved ciphers only (AES-GCM variants)
If MD5 or weak algorithms are available, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Configure OpenSSL FIPS property filtering and nginx SSL settings:
1. Edit /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf 2. Add algorithm section with FIPS property filtering: [algorithm_sect] default_properties = fips=yes
3. Configure nginx /etc/nginx/nginx.conf: ssl_protocols TLSv1.2 TLSv1.3; ssl_ciphers 'ECDHE-ECDSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:ECDHE-RSA-AES256-GCM-SHA384:...';
4. Verify MD5 blocking: echo -n 'test' | openssl dgst -md5 5. Verify TLS 1.0/1.1 cannot negotiate connections 6. Verify only 14 FIPS cipher suites available
Rebuild container with proper openssl.cnf and nginx.conf configuration.

Rule.fixtext: Configure algorithm blocking for the container

reference.dc:title: Test Execution Summary
reference.dc:subject: Test 1: TLS Protocol Tests & Test 2: FIPS Cipher Tests

Group.title: SRG-OS-000342-GPOS-00133
Group.description: The Debian operating system must generate audit records for privileged activities.

Rule.title: Debian must log security-relevant events.
Rule.description:
Audit logging ensures accountability and enables detection of security violations.
Container Implementation: - Entrypoint validation: docker-entrypoint.sh - FIPS POST execution: wolfSSL Known Answer Tests on every startup - OpenSSL provider verification: wolfProvider status check - Nginx configuration validation: nginx -t on startup - Container logs: docker logs (stdout/stderr) - Access logs: /var/log/nginx/access.log - Error logs: /var/log/nginx/error.log

check.check-content:
Verify audit logging is configured:
1. Check entrypoint validation script exists: $ docker exec <container> cat /docker-entrypoint.sh | grep "FIPS"
Expected: FIPS POST execution and provider verification present
2. View container startup logs: $ docker logs <container-id>
Expected events: - ✓ Running wolfSSL FIPS POST (Known Answer Tests) - ✓ FIPS POST completed successfully - ✓ wolfProvider loaded and active - ✓ FIPS enforcement enabled (fips=yes) - ✓ Nginx configuration is valid
3. Check nginx access logging: $ docker exec <container> cat /etc/nginx/nginx.conf | grep access_log
Expected: access_log configured with SSL protocol and cipher logging
If logging is not configured or events are not logged, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Configure audit logging:
1. Ensure docker-entrypoint.sh executes FIPS validation checks 2. Ensure wolfSSL FIPS POST runs on startup 3. Ensure OpenSSL provider verification executes 4. Ensure nginx -t validates configuration 5. Configure access_log with SSL details 6. Verify all validation output goes to stdout/stderr 7. Container should exit on validation failure (fail-fast)
Rebuild container with proper entrypoint and logging configuration.

Rule.fixtext: Configure audit logging for the container

Group.title: SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00227
Group.description: The Debian operating system must verify package integrity.

Rule.title: Debian must verify package and library integrity.
Rule.description:
Package integrity verification ensures that software has not been tampered with.
Container Implementation: - wolfSSL FIPS: In-core integrity hash validated via fips-hash.sh during build - FIPS POST: Known Answer Tests validate cryptographic module on every startup - SBOM generated: SPDX 2.3 format - Image signed: Cosign signature - VEX statement: Vulnerability tracking - SLSA provenance: Build attestation

check.check-content:
Verify package integrity:
1. Verify FIPS POST execution: $ docker logs <container> 2>&1 | grep "FIPS POST"
Expected: ✓ FIPS POST completed successfully
2. Check OpenSSL provider: $ docker exec <container> openssl list -providers
Expected: wolfSSL Provider FIPS v1.1.0 (status: active)
3. Check for SBOM: $ ls supply-chain/SBOM-nginx-1.27.3-debian-bookworm-fips.spdx.json
Expected: File exists
4. Verify image signature: $ cosign verify nginx:1.27.3-debian-bookworm-fips
Expected: Signature valid
If integrity cannot be verified, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Configure package integrity verification:
1. Execute fips-hash.sh during wolfSSL build 2. Run FIPS POST (Known Answer Tests) on every container startup 3. Verify wolfProvider loads successfully 4. Generate SPDX SBOM 5. Generate VEX statement 6. Generate SLSA provenance 7. Sign image with Cosign
Rebuild container with integrity verification enabled.

Group.title: SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00228
Group.description: The Debian operating system must run processes as a non-root user where possible.

Rule.title: Debian must enforce least privilege principle.
Rule.description:
Running processes as non-root limits the impact of security vulnerabilities.
Container Implementation: - Nginx master process: Runs as root (required for port 80/443 binding) - Nginx worker processes: Run as nginx user (non-root) - Container user directive: USER instruction may be used for non-privileged ports - Capability dropping: Container runtime can drop unnecessary capabilities
Note: Nginx requires root for privileged ports (80, 443) but worker processes run as unprivileged user. This is standard nginx architecture.

check.check-content:
Verify nginx worker processes run as non-root:
1. Check nginx configuration for user directive: $ docker exec <container> grep "^user" /etc/nginx/nginx.conf
Expected: user nginx; (or similar non-root user)
2. Verify worker processes at runtime: $ docker exec <container> ps aux | grep nginx
Expected: - Master process: root (required for privileged ports) - Worker processes: nginx user (non-root)
3. For non-privileged ports, verify container user: $ docker inspect nginx:1.27.3-debian-bookworm-fips | grep User
If all processes run as root without justification, this is a finding.

fix.fixtext:
Configure non-root execution:
1. Add user directive to nginx.conf: user nginx; 2. Ensure nginx user exists in container 3. For non-privileged ports: Use USER instruction in Dockerfile 4. Drop unnecessary capabilities at runtime 5. Verify: docker exec container ps aux | grep nginx
Rebuild container with proper user configuration.

Rule.fixtext: Configure non-root user for the container

Group.title: SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00229
Group.description: The Debian operating system must restrict file permissions.

Rule.title: Debian must restrict file permissions to prevent unauthorized access.
Rule.description:
Proper file permissions prevent unauthorized modification of system files.
Container Implementation: - wolfSSL libraries: 0644 (libwolfssl.so*) - wolfProvider: 0644 (wolfprov.so) - Configuration: 0644 (openssl.cnf, nginx.conf) - Scripts: 0755 (docker-entrypoint.sh) - SSL certificates: 0644 (self-signed.crt) - SSL private keys: 0600 (self-signed.key) - No world-writable files

check.check-content:
Verify file permissions:
1. Check for world-writable files: $ docker run --rm nginx:1.27.3-debian-bookworm-fips find / -type f -perm -002 2>/dev/null
Expected: No output (no world-writable files)
2. Check sensitive file permissions: $ docker exec <container> ls -la /usr/local/lib/libwolfssl.so* $ docker exec <container> ls -la /usr/local/openssl/lib64/ossl-modules/wolfprov.so $ docker exec <container> ls -la /etc/ssl/openssl.cnf $ docker exec <container> ls -la /etc/nginx/nginx.conf $ docker exec <container> ls -la /etc/nginx/ssl/self-signed.key
Expected: - Libraries: -rw-r--r-- (0644) - Configuration: -rw-r--r-- (0644) - Scripts: -rwxr-xr-x (0755) - Private keys: -rw------- (0600)
If world-writable files exist or permissions are incorrect, this is a finding.

Rule.fixtext: Configure file permissions for the container

Group.title: SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00230
Group.description: Kernel module loading restrictions (NOT APPLICABLE - Host Responsibility)

Rule.title: Kernel module loading (N/A for containers)
Rule.description:
NOT APPLICABLE: Containers share the host kernel and cannot load kernel modules independently. This control is the responsibility of the container host.
Justification: - Containers use the host kernel - No independent kernel module loading capability - Host-level security control
Mitigation: Deploy container on STIG-compliant host with proper kernel hardening.
Rule.check-content: This check is not applicable to containers. Verify host compliance instead.

fix.fixtext: Configure kernel module restrictions on container host.

Group.title: SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00231
Group.description: Boot loader configuration (NOT APPLICABLE - No Boot Process)

Rule.title: Boot loader password (N/A for containers)
Rule.description:
NOT APPLICABLE: Containers do not have boot loaders. They are started by container runtime.
Justification: - Containers don't boot; they are started by runtime (Docker, containerd) - No GRUB or boot loader present - Process execution, not boot sequence
Mitigation: N/A - containers use runtime start, not boot.
Rule.check-content: This check is not applicable to containers. No boot process exists.

fix.fixtext: N/A - containers do not have boot loaders.

Group.title: SRG-OS-000480-GPOS-00232
Group.description: Systemd service hardening (NOT APPLICABLE - Minimal Container)

Rule.title: Systemd hardening (N/A for containers)
Rule.description:
NOT APPLICABLE: Minimal containers use entrypoint scripts, not systemd.
Justification: - Minimal container uses entrypoint (/docker-entrypoint.sh) - No systemd or init system - Single-purpose application container
Mitigation: Process supervision handled by container runtime (Docker, Kubernetes).
Rule.check-content: This check is not applicable to containers. No systemd present.

fix.fixtext: Use container runtime supervision instead of systemd.
